intent: Build running_total(nums): given a list of numbers, return a new list where element i is the sum of nums[0] through nums[i] (a running cumulative sum). For example running_total([1, 2, 3]) returns [1, 3, 6]. An empty list returns an empty list.
oracle_test:
from running_total import running_total


def test_oracle():
    assert running_total([1, 2, 3]) == [1, 3, 6]
    assert running_total([]) == []
    assert running_total([5]) == [5]
    assert running_total([-1, 1, -1]) == [-1, 0, -1]
    assert running_total([10, 0, 0]) == [10, 10, 10]
